title: The Shadow Author's Journal
description: Hidden journal entries found throughout the Tome. Reveals the Shadow Author's inner thoughts.

entries.title: The First Page
entries.text: They came. Three of them. An archer, a girl, and an old man. I thought they'd be afraid. Instead, the archer SHOT at me. The audacity. Don't they know I wrote their endings?

entries.title: The Wizard
entries.text: Merlin saw through me. Of course he did — he's eight hundred years old. But he's trapped in crystal, and crystal doesn't judge. Much.

entries.title: The Wild Man
entries.text: Tarzan doesn't speak much. But when he does, every word hits like a fist. He called me 'lost.' I am NOT lost. I am the one who WRITES the paths.

entries.title: The Vampire
entries.text: Dracula chose to stop feeding. Three hundred years of darkness, and he CHOSE the light. How? How does a monster choose to be good? I've been trying for eight hundred years and I still can't.

entries.title: The Monster
entries.text: Frankenstein's creature said something today that broke me. 'You... freed me? No one has ever...' He THANKED me for imprisoning him. Because even a cage is better than being alone. I understand that. God help me, I understand that.

entries.title: The Boy
entries.text: Peter Pan grew up today. Not physically — he's still a boy. But he chose to stay and fight instead of flying away to Neverland. He chose his friends over his paradise. Why can't I do that?

entries.title: The Hook
entries.text: Captain Hook lowered his sword. For the first time in a hundred years, he stopped fighting. 'I'm tired of being the villain,' he said. I wanted to say 'me too.' But I didn't.

entries.title: The Music
entries.text: The Phantom played his organ tonight. The sound traveled through every page, every chapter, every word I've ever written. And for one moment — one beautiful, terrible moment — I remembered what it felt like to be PART of a story instead of ABOVE it.

entries.title: The Last Entry
entries.text: They're coming for me. All of them. Together. I should be afraid. But I'm not. I'm... relieved. Eight hundred years of writing stories, and I never got one of my own. Maybe this is it. Maybe this is my story. The villain who wanted to be loved.

entries.title: After (if redeemed)
entries.text: They forgave me. I don't understand why. I stole their stories, their freedom, their worlds. And they forgave me. The girl — Alice — she said 'Everyone deserves a happy ending. Even the author.' I'm crying as I write this. For the first time in eight hundred years, these are not ink tears.
